responsibilities: Perform signal integrity simulations on high-speed interfaces such as DDR, PCIe, and SerDes to validate timing and signal quality.
Conduct power integrity analysis including PDN impedance, decoupling capacitor placement, and voltage drop simulation on PCB designs.
Analyze S-parameters, eye diagrams, and crosstalk to ensure signal margins meet specification across process and temperature corners.
Collaborate with PCB layout engineers to define stackup, impedance targets, and routing guidelines for high-speed boards.
Run pre-layout and post-layout SI/PI simulations using Cadence Sigrity, HyperLynx, or Ansys SIwave to catch design issues early.
Debug signal integrity issues on physical boards using high-speed oscilloscopes, TDR measurements, and vector network analyzers.
Develop channel models and perform IBIS-AMI simulations to validate high-speed serial link performance and jitter budgets.
Review board stackup and via structures to minimize impedance discontinuities and electromagnetic interference on critical nets.
Provide design guidelines and constraints to PCB designers for differential pairs, length matching, and via stitching.
Document signal and power integrity analysis results, correlating simulation data with lab measurements for design sign-off.
